dialogos.texto_en: Oh, Ichika.\nWhat are you looking at over there?
Hey, Itsuki-chan and Futaro-kun.\nWhy are you calling out to me?
Why?\nCause we saw you.
Aren't you on a date?
A d-date!
Well, you do know that there are seven of us on this trip, right?
Th-that's right!
And? Ichika, what were you looking around for?
It's not like I was looking for anything in particular.\nI just thought there were a lot of souvenir shops,\nbut there are quite a few food shops too.
Well, it's the main street of Naha,\nso I'm sure they have everything.
I think there's a market nearby\nthat's even called the kitchen of Okinawa.
The kitchen of Okinawa, huh?\nI wonder if they sell goya there?
What a cliche idea...
It's fine, isn't it?\nGoya is what comes to mind\nwhen you think of Okinawan cuisine.
Indeed, goya champuru is especially famous\namong Okinawan dishes.
By the way, goya is rich in vitamin C\nand can also help prevent heatstroke.
Oh, cool.\nIt's the perfect vegetable for Okinawa.
You know a lot about it, Itsuki.
Well, since it's our Okinawa trip,\nI did some research beforehand.
Okaay!\nItsuki-chan, who's done her homework,\nI have a question for you.
Sure, what is it?
Goya champuru is famous,\nbut what other goya dishes are there?
...Ummm?
You haven't researched that, have you?
I can't think of anything else right away\nbecause goya champuru is so dominant in my mind...
Somen champuru is another one,\nbut that doesn't use goya.
I think champuru means something like a mixture.\nIt refers to the state of being cooked altogether.
I see.\nSo it's called champuru because it's a stir-fry dish\nwith various ingredients mixed together, right?
So, goya champuru is just a champuru\nwhere goya is the main ingredient?
Goya is a pretty bitter vegetable,\nisn't it?\nIt seems difficult to use it well in cooking.
Mmh...\nI don't cook, so I have no idea what to imagine.
In that sense,\nNino might be able to do something about it.
It would be fun to buy some goya\nand ask her to make something with it.
Indeed, she could probably come up\nwith some delicious dish, with a strange name.
N-no, I'll make it!
Huh?
I can't just leave it all to Nino!\nLet me feed you too, Ues-...\nFutaro.
Ooh?\nThis is...
What do you think?\nWould you like to try my cooking?
Make sure you cook up something edible, yeah?\nI can't imagine you cooking.
How rude!\nI could do it if I put my mind to it!
Ohhh?\nI'm really looking forward to it then.
...Well, I DO think I can do it,\nbut I don't want to be judged based on Nino's cooking.
You suddenly seem unsure of yourself!
I'd like to try your cooking, Itsuki.
Leave it to me!\nI'll make goya taste delicious for you, Futaro!
That's a high bar to set, you know?\nGoya is difficult to cook.
No worries, I'll do my best!
It's not exactly a problem that can \nbe solved with determination, you know?
In that case, I'll cook, too.
Wha...? Why is that?
Well, if I move to Tokyo, I'll be living alone.\nIt's convenient to be able to cook for myself.
So, how about we cook for each other?
For each other...
It'll be good practice to have another person taste it.
Yes! Let's definitely do that!
Let's do our best together.\nFu-... Futaro.
Itsuki-chan, you're becoming quite overly eager.\nSuddenly talking about making homemade food.
Futaro, is there anything else you want to eat?\nI'll make whatever you like.
Whatever I like...\nAs long as it's edible, it's fine.
Come on now, don't be rude, Futaro-kun.\nDepending on how you say that,\nit can be extremely disrespectful.
Indeed. But, I understand, so it's okay.
Oh? Itsuki-chan understands.\nAs expected of his girlfriend.\nSuch composure.
Th-that's not what I meant!\nIchika, you understand too, right?!
Aww, you're so cute, Itsuki-chan.\nRight, Futaro-kun?
Don't put me on the spot...
